channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Most US Institute of Peace workers get late-night word of their mass firing
item.description: Most employees at the U.S. Institute of Peace, a congressionally created and funded think tank now taken over by Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency, have received email notices of their mass firing — the latest step in the Trump administration’s government downsizing.
item.dc:creator: Gary Fields And Matthew Lee, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Richard Carlbom ascends to top role at Minnesota DFL Party as its first new chair in 14 years
item.description: Richard Carlbom was chosen as the Minnesota DFL Party chair, rising to the top post after years of working behind the scenes on party elections and causes.
item.dc:creator: MPR News Staff, MPR News

item.title: WATCH: Dems struggle to name the leader of the Democratic Party
item.description: Top voices in the Democratic Party, as well as average Americans, have struggled to answer who the party's leader is when asked in interviews.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump draws laughs when defining a 'woman' — until he touches on a serious issue
item.description: When asked to define a "woman," President Donald Trump started with funny lines before turning to a serious issue, the inclusion of transgender athletes in women's sports, which he called "crazy.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Vance accuses Denmark of underinvesting in Greenland as Trump presses for US takeover of the island
item.description: NUUK, Greenland (AP) — U.S. Vice President JD Vance said Friday that Denmark has "underinvested" in Greenland’s security and demanded that Denmark change its approach as President Donald Trump pushes to take over the Danish territory. The pointed remarks came as Vance visited U.S.
item.dc:creator: PHILIP CROWTHER, KIRSTEN GRIESHABER and AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Appeals court rules DOGE can continue operating at USAID in another win for Trump administration
item.description: A federal appeals court on Friday ruled the Department of Government Efficiency may continue operating at the United States Agency for International Development.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Red Lake Nation leader condemns Trump orders during State of the Band Address
item.description: During the Red Lake Nation State of the Band Address Friday, chair Darrell Seki Jr., condemned President Donald Trump’s recent executive orders, saying they have created a crisis throughout Indian Country.
item.dc:creator: Mathew Holding Eagle III, MPR News

item.title: Trump signs executive order to end collective bargaining at agencies involved with national security
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump moved Thursday to end collective bargaining with federal labor unions in agencies with national security missions across the federal government, citing authority granted him under a 1978 law.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The Minnesota Democratic-Farmer-Labor Party will elect a new chair on Saturday
item.description: Two Democrats are vying to lead the state’s DFL Party. Members of the DFL will vote Saturday for a replacement to longtime party chair Ken Martin who left earlier this year to head up the Democratic National Committee.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: Trump administration investigates Maine for claims of withholding gender transitions from parents
item.description: PORTLAND, Maine (AP) — The administration of President Donald Trump is investigating Maine with a claim that dozens of school districts in the state violate federal law by withholding information about students' gender transitioning from parents. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Major law firm reaches deal with Trump to avoid White House order even as two other firms sue
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A prominent international law firm reached a deal with President Donald Trump on Friday to dedicate at least $100 million in free legal services and to review its hiring practices, averting a punishing executive order like the ones directed at nearly a half-dozen other major legal
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Kentucky Gov. Andy Beshear displays his colors in veto fights with Republican lawmakers
item.description: FRANKFORT, Ky. (AP) — Knowing the odds of winning were stacked against him, Democratic Gov. Andy Beshear nevertheless vetoed bills sent his way by the Republican-dominated Kentucky legislature with by-now familiar themes: abortion, Medicaid and diversity initiatives.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SCHREINER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: McConnell: U.S. headed for ‘Russia wins, America loses’ headline on Ukraine deal
item.description: Former Senate Republican Leader Mitch McConnell (Ky.) warned in a speech Thursday that U.S. negotiators trying to end the war in Ukraine are headed for a “Russia wins, America loses” headline if they agree to a deal that achieves only “illusory peace.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump holds 'productive call' with new Canadian prime minister
item.description: President Trump spoke with Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney on Friday for the first time since Carney was elected earlier in the month amid tensions between the two nations.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: GOP leaders accused of making threats to block bill to let new moms vote remotely
item.description: Lawmakers from both parties teamed up to force a House vote on a measure allowing new parents to vote by proxy for 12 weeks, but House Speaker Mike Johnson opposes it on Constitutional grounds.
item.dc:creator: Deirdre Walsh, Claudia Grisales, and Barbara Sprunt, MPR News

item.title: European institutions target 'scientific asylum-seekers' from US
item.description: The move comes as federal funds for research are cut and studies are subject to ideological limitations.
item.dc:creator: Steph Whiteside, NewsNation

item.title: Trump administration says it will pull back billions in COVID funding from local health departments
item.description: Federal health officials said Tuesday they are pulling back $11.4 billion in COVID-19-related funds for local public health departments and other health organizations throughout the nation. Minnesota stands to lose $226 million.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press and Erica Zurek, MPR News

item.title: Appeals court won't lift Boasberg's order blocking Alien Enemies Act
item.description: A federal appeals court in a 2-1 decision Wednesday refused to lift U.S. District Judge James Boasberg’s order blocking the Trump administration from swiftly deporting migrants under the Alien Enemies Act. The Justice Department had urged the three-judge panel on the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, Fox 5

item.title: NPR and PBS heads answer questions on Capitol Hill regarding allegations of bias
item.description: The CEOs of the largest U.S. public broadcasting networks appeared before a House subcommittee chaired by Republican Rep. Marjorie Taylor Greene.
item.dc:creator: Scott Neuman and David Folkenflik, MPR News

item.title: Nevada Gov. Joe Lombardo talks possible Medicaid cuts, education, movie studios
item.description: Republican Nevada Gov. Joe Lombardo said he may have to call a special session later in the year to handle possible cuts from the federal government that directly impact the state budget.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News
